name: Valuatron

consumes.minimum: 1 active comp + 1 selected layer + 1 selected property that canVaryOverTime
consumes.description: Reads the active CompItem for its current timeline time (comp.time), iterates comp.selectedLayers, and for each layer iterates layer.selectedProperties. Reads prop.canVaryOverTime and prop.value at the current time before writing the keyframe.

functions: validate that an active composition is present
validate that at least one layer is selected
open a single undo group named 'Valuatron'
iterate every selected layer and every selected property on that layer
skip properties where canVaryOverTime is false
sample each keyframeable property's current evaluated value via prop.value
write a keyframe at comp.time with that value via prop.setValueAtTime
surface per-property errors via alert without aborting remaining properties

scriptSim.scenario: success — create keyframes for two selected properties at current time
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Main Comp' (1920×1080, 10 s, 24 fps); 1 selected layer 'Shape Layer 1'; 2 selected properties: Transform/Position ([960,540]) and Transform/Opacity (75); playhead at t=2 s

notes: HEADLESS — no dialog or panel; runs immediately when the script file is executed.
Only properties where canVaryOverTime === true receive a keyframe; group properties (e.g. Transform) are silently skipped.
The keyframe value is prop.value — the evaluated value at comp.time, which includes any expression result rather than the underlying static value.
Per-property errors are reported via alert() but do not abort processing of remaining properties.
All mutations are wrapped in a single undo group so Cmd+Z / Ctrl+Z reverses everything in one step.
writeLn() calls for layer progress are non-mutating and do not appear in the operation log.
